name: Ability Check
description: An ability check is a D20 Test that represents using one of the six abilities -- or a specific skill associated with an ability -- to overcome a challenge.

name: Ability Score and Modifier
description: A creature has six ability scores -- Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma -- each of which has a corresponding modifier. Add the modifier when you make a D20 Test with the corresponding ability or when a rule asks you to do so.

name: Action
description: On your turn, you can take one action. Choose which action to take from those available or from the special actions provided by your features. Available actions: Attack, Dash, Disengage, Dodge, Help, Hide, Influence, Magic, Ready, Search, Study, Utilize.

name: Advantage
description: If you have Advantage on a D20 Test, roll two d20s, and use the higher roll. A roll can't be affected by more than one Advantage, and Advantage and Disadvantage on the same roll cancel each other.

name: Adventure
description: An adventure is a series of encounters. A story emerges through playing them.

name: Alignment
description: A creature's alignment broadly describes its ethical attitudes and ideals. Alignment is a combination of two factors: one identifies morality (good, evil, or neutral), and the other describes attitudes toward order (lawful, chaotic, or neutral). These factors allow for nine possible combinations, such as Lawful Good and Neutral Evil.

name: Ally
description: A creature is your ally if it is a member of your adventuring party, your friend, on your side in combat, or a creature that the rules or the GM designates as your ally.

name: Area of Effect
description: The descriptions of many spells and other features specify that they have an area of effect, which typically has one of six shapes: Cone, Cube, Cylinder, Emanation, Line, or Sphere. An area of effect has a point of origin, a location from which the effect's energy erupts. If all straight lines extending from the point of origin to a location in the area of effect are blocked, that location isn't included in the area of effect. To block a line, an obstruction must provide Total Cover.

name: Armor Class
description: An Armor Class (AC) is the target number for an attack roll. AC represents how difficult it is to hit a target. Your base AC calculation is 10 plus your Dexterity modifier. If a rule gives you another base AC calculation, you choose which calculation to use; you can't use more than one.

name: Armor Training
description: Armor training allows you to use armor of a certain category without drawbacks. If you wear Light, Medium, or Heavy armor and lack training with it, you have Disadvantage on any D20 Test that involves Strength or Dexterity, and you can't cast spells. If you use a Shield and lack training with it, you don't gain its AC bonus.

name: Attack
description: Action. When you take the Attack action, you can make one attack roll with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike. You can either equip or unequip one weapon when you make an attack as part of this action. If you move on your turn and have a feature that gives you more than one attack, you can use some or all of that movement to move between those attacks.

name: Attack Roll
description: An attack roll is a D20 Test that represents making an attack with a weapon, an Unarmed Strike, or a spell.

name: Attitude
description: A monster has a starting attitude toward a player character: Friendly, Hostile, or Indifferent.

name: Attunement
description: Some magic items require a creature to form a bond -- called Attunement -- with them before the creature can use an item's magical properties. A creature can have Attunement with no more than three magic items at a time.

name: Blinded
description: Condition. Can't See: You can't see and automatically fail any ability check that requires sight. Attacks Affected: Attack rolls against you have Advantage, and your attack rolls have Disadvantage.

name: Blindsight
description: If you have Blindsight, you can see within a specific range without relying on physical sight. Within that range, you can see anything that isn't behind Total Cover even if you have the Blinded condition or are in Darkness. Moreover, in that range, you can see something that has the Invisible condition.

name: Bloodied
description: A creature is Bloodied while it has half its Hit Points or fewer remaining.

name: Bonus Action
description: A Bonus Action is a special action that you can take on the same turn that you take an action. You can't take more than one Bonus Action on a turn, and you have a Bonus Action to take only if a rule explicitly says so.

name: Breaking Objects
description: Objects can be harmed by attacks and by some spells. Object AC: Cloth/paper/rope 11, Crystal/glass/ice 13, Wood 15, Stone 17, Iron/steel 19, Mithral 21, Adamantine 23. Object HP: Tiny fragile 2 (1d4) / resilient 5 (2d4), Small fragile 3 (1d6) / resilient 10 (3d6), Medium fragile 4 (1d8) / resilient 18 (4d8), Large fragile 5 (1d10) / resilient 27 (5d10). Objects have Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage.

name: Bright Light
description: Bright Light is normal illumination.

name: Burning
description: Hazard. A burning creature or object takes 1d4 Fire damage at the start of each of its turns. As an action, you can extinguish fire on yourself by giving yourself the Prone condition and rolling on the ground. The fire also goes out if it is doused, submerged, or suffocated.

name: Burrow Speed
description: A creature that has a Burrow Speed can use that speed to move through sand, earth, mud, or ice. The creature can't burrow through solid rock unless the creature has a trait that allows it to do so.

name: Campaign
description: A campaign is a series of adventures.

name: Cantrip
description: A cantrip is a level 0 spell, which is cast without a spell slot.

name: Carrying Capacity
description: Your size and Strength score determine the maximum weight in pounds that you can carry. Tiny: carry Str x 7.5 lb. Small/Medium: carry Str x 15 lb. Large: carry Str x 30 lb. Huge: carry Str x 60 lb. Gargantuan: carry Str x 120 lb. While dragging/lifting/pushing weight in excess of the maximum, your Speed can be no more than 5 feet.

name: Challenge Rating
description: Challenge Rating (CR) summarizes the threat a monster poses to a group of four player characters. If the CR is higher than the characters' level, the monster is likely a danger.

name: Character Sheet
description: A character sheet is a paper or digital record that you use to track your character's information.

name: Charmed
description: Condition. Can't Harm the Charmer: You can't attack the charmer or target the charmer with damaging abilities or magical effects. Social Advantage: The charmer has Advantage on any ability check to interact with you socially.

name: Climbing
description: While you're climbing, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot (2 extra feet in Difficult Terrain). You ignore this extra cost if you have a Climb Speed and use it to climb. Climbing a slippery surface might require a successful DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check.

name: Climb Speed
description: A Climb Speed can be used in place of Speed to traverse a vertical surface without expending the extra movement normally associated with climbing.

name: Concentration
description: Some spells and other effects require Concentration to remain active. Broken by: Another Concentration Effect, Damage (Constitution saving throw, DC 10 or half damage taken, whichever is higher, max DC 30), Incapacitated or Dead.

name: Condition
description: A condition is a temporary game state. Conditions: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Exhaustion, Frightened, Grappled, Incapacitated, Invisible, Paralyzed, Petrified, Poisoned, Prone, Restrained, Stunned, Unconscious. A condition doesn't stack with itself except Exhaustion.

name: Cone
description: Area of Effect. A Cone extends in straight lines from a point of origin in a direction its creator chooses. A Cone's width at any point along its length equals that point's distance from the point of origin.

name: Cover
description: Cover provides protection. Half Cover: +2 bonus to AC and Dexterity saving throws. Three-Quarters Cover: +5 bonus to AC and Dexterity saving throws. Total Cover: can't be targeted directly.

name: Crawling
description: While you're crawling, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot (2 extra feet in Difficult Terrain).

name: Creature
description: Any being in the game, including a player's character, is a creature.

name: Creature Type
description: Every creature has a type: Aberration, Beast, Celestial, Construct, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Giant, Humanoid, Monstrosity, Ooze, Plant, Undead. Most player characters are Humanoid.

name: Critical Hit
description: If you roll a 20 on the d20 for an attack roll, you score a Critical Hit, and the attack hits regardless of any modifiers or the target's AC. Roll all of the attack's damage dice twice and add them together, then add any relevant modifiers.

name: Cube
description: Area of Effect. A Cube extends in straight lines from a point of origin located anywhere on a face of the Cube. The effect specifies its size, which is the length of each side.

name: Curses
description: Some game effects curse a creature or an object. Curses can be removed by the Remove Curse and Greater Restoration spells or other magic that explicitly ends curses.

name: Cylinder
description: Area of Effect. A Cylinder extends from a point of origin at the center of the circular top or bottom. The effect specifies the radius and height. A Cylinder's point of origin is included in the area of effect.

name: D20 Test
description: D20 Tests encompass the three main d20 rolls: ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws. If something affects D20 Tests, it affects all three.

name: Damage
description: Damage represents harm that causes a creature or an object to lose Hit Points.

name: Damage Roll
description: A damage roll is a die roll, adjusted by any applicable modifiers, that deals damage to a target.

name: Damage Threshold
description: A creature or object with a damage threshold has Immunity to all damage unless it takes an amount from a single attack or effect equal to or greater than its threshold, in which case it takes that entire instance of damage.

name: Damage Types
description: Types: Acid, Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, Necrotic, Piercing, Poison, Psychic, Radiant, Slashing, Thunder. Damage types have no rules of their own, but other rules such as Resistance rely on them.

name: Darkness
description: An area of Darkness is Heavily Obscured.

name: Darkvision
description: If you have Darkvision, you can see in Dim Light within a specified range as if it were Bright Light and in Darkness within that range as if it were Dim Light. You discern colors in that Darkness only as shades of gray.

name: Dash
description: Action. When you take the Dash action, you gain extra movement for the current turn. The increase equals your Speed after applying any modifiers.

name: Dead
description: A dead creature has no Hit Points and can't regain them unless revived by magic such as Raise Dead or Revivify. The spirit knows who is casting and can refuse. If the creature returns to life, it returns with any ongoing conditions, magical contagions, or curses. If it died with Exhaustion levels, it returns with 1 fewer level.

name: Deafened
description: Condition. Can't Hear: You can't hear and automatically fail any ability check that requires hearing.

name: Death Saving Throw
description: A player character must make a Death Saving Throw if they start their turn with 0 Hit Points.

name: Dehydration
description: Hazard. Water needs per day: Tiny 1/4 gallon, Small 1 gallon, Medium 1 gallon, Large 4 gallons, Huge 16 gallons, Gargantuan 64 gallons. A creature that drinks less than half gains 1 Exhaustion level at day's end.

name: Difficult Terrain
description: Every foot of movement in Difficult Terrain costs 1 extra foot. Caused by: non-Tiny non-ally creatures, large furniture, heavy snow/ice/rubble/undergrowth, shin-to-waist-deep liquid, narrow openings, slopes of 20+ degrees.

name: Difficulty Class
description: A Difficulty Class (DC) is the target number for an ability check or a saving throw.

name: Dim Light
description: An area with Dim Light is Lightly Obscured.

name: Disadvantage
description: If you have Disadvantage on a D20 Test, roll two d20s and use the lower roll. Advantage and Disadvantage on the same roll cancel each other.

name: Disengage
description: Action. If you take the Disengage action, your movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks for the rest of the current turn.

name: Dodge
description: Action. Until the start of your next turn, any attack roll made against you has Disadvantage if you can see the attacker, and you make Dexterity saving throws with Advantage. You lose these benefits if Incapacitated or Speed is 0.

name: Emanation
description: Area of Effect. An Emanation extends from a creature or object in all directions. It moves with its origin unless instantaneous or stationary.

name: Encounter
description: An encounter is a scene in an adventure involving social interaction, exploration, or combat.

name: Enemy
description: A creature is your enemy if it fights against you in combat, actively works to harm you, or is designated as your enemy by the rules or GM.

name: Exhaustion
description: Condition. Cumulative. Each time received, gain 1 level. Die at level 6. D20 Tests reduced by 2 x level. Speed reduced by 5 x level feet. Finishing a Long Rest removes 1 level.

name: Experience Points
description: Characters earn Experience Points (XP) by overcoming challenges. When XP crosses certain thresholds, the character's level increases.

name: Expertise
description: When you make an ability check with a skill in which you have Expertise, your Proficiency Bonus is doubled for that check. You can't have Expertise in the same skill more than once.

name: Falling
description: Hazard. A creature that falls takes 1d6 Bludgeoning damage per 10 feet fallen, max 20d6. It has the Prone condition unless it avoids all damage. Falling into water: DC 15 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check to halve damage.

name: Flying
description: While flying, you fall if Incapacitated, Prone, or Fly Speed is reduced to 0. You can stay aloft if you can hover.

name: Fly Speed
description: A Fly Speed can be used to travel through the air. While you have a Fly Speed, you can stay aloft until you land, fall, or die.

name: Friendly
description: Attitude. A Friendly creature views you favorably. You have Advantage on ability checks to influence it.

name: Frightened
description: Condition. Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of fear is within line of sight. Can't willingly move closer to the source of fear.

name: Grappled
description: Condition. Speed 0, can't increase. Disadvantage on attack rolls against any target other than the grappler. The grappler can drag or carry you, costing 1 extra foot per foot unless you are Tiny or two+ sizes smaller.

name: Grappling
description: A creature can grapple another using an Unarmed Strike. One grapple per hand. Ending: Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check vs. escape DC, or grappler becomes Incapacitated, or distance exceeds range. Grappler can release at any time (no action).

name: Hazard
description: A hazard is an environmental danger. Types: Burning, Dehydration, Falling, Malnutrition, Suffocation.

name: Healing
description: Healing is how you regain Hit Points.

name: Heavily Obscured
description: You have the Blinded condition while trying to see something in a Heavily Obscured space.

name: Help
description: Action. Assist an Ability Check: Choose a skill/tool proficiency and an ally; they get Advantage on their next check with it (expires at start of your next turn). Assist an Attack Roll: Distract an enemy within 5 feet; an ally gets Advantage on next attack roll against it.

name: Heroic Inspiration
description: If you have Heroic Inspiration, you can expend it to reroll any die immediately after rolling it, and you must use the new roll. If you gain it but already have it, it's lost unless you give it to another player character who lacks it.

name: Hide
description: Action. Succeed on DC 15 Dexterity (Stealth) check while Heavily Obscured or behind Three-Quarters/Total Cover and out of enemy line of sight. On success, you have the Invisible condition. Your check total is the DC for creatures to find you. Ends when you make a sound louder than a whisper, are found, make an attack roll, or cast a spell with a Verbal component.

name: High Jump
description: Leap into the air a number of feet equal to 3 + Strength modifier (min 0) if you move at least 10 feet first. Standing: half distance. Each foot costs a foot of movement. You can reach the jump height plus 1-1/2 times your height.

name: Hit Point Dice
description: Hit Dice help determine a player character's Hit Point maximum. A creature can spend Hit Dice during a Short Rest to regain Hit Points.

name: Hit Points
description: Hit Points (HP) measure how difficult it is to kill or destroy a creature or object. Damage reduces HP, healing restores them. Can't exceed maximum or go below 0.

name: Hostile
description: Attitude. A Hostile creature views you unfavorably. You have Disadvantage on ability checks to influence it.

name: Hover
description: Some creatures can hover. Hovering while flying prevents you from falling in certain circumstances.

name: Illusions
description: Magical illusions are insubstantial and weightless, yet seem affected by the environment as if real unless the creating effect specifies otherwise.

name: Immunity
description: If you have Immunity to a damage type or a condition, it doesn't affect you in any way.

name: Improvised Weapons
description: An object wielded as a makeshift weapon. Don't add Proficiency Bonus to attack rolls. Deals 1d4 damage. If thrown: range 20/60. The GM may allow it to function as a similar weapon.

name: Incapacitated
description: Condition. Inactive: Can't take any action, Bonus Action, or Reaction. No Concentration. Speechless: Can't speak. Surprised: Disadvantage on Initiative if Incapacitated when you roll it.

name: Indifferent
description: Attitude. An Indifferent creature has no desire to help or hinder you. Default attitude of a monster.

name: Influence
description: Action. Urge a monster to do something. Willing: no check needed. Unwilling: won't comply. Hesitant: ability check required, DC 15 or monster's Intelligence, whichever is higher. Checks: Deception, Intimidation, Performance, Persuasion, or Animal Handling.

name: Initiative
description: Determines the order of turns during combat. Initiative score = 10 + Dexterity modifier. Advantage on Initiative rolls: +5. Disadvantage: -5.

name: Invisible
description: Condition. Advantage on Initiative rolls. Not affected by effects requiring sight (unless creator can see you). Attack rolls against you have Disadvantage, your attack rolls have Advantage.

name: Jumping
description: When you jump, you make either a Long Jump (horizontal) or a High Jump (vertical).

name: Knocking Out a Creature
description: When you would reduce a creature to 0 HP with a melee attack, you can instead reduce it to 1 HP. It gains the Unconscious condition and starts a Short Rest. Remains Unconscious until it regains HP or someone succeeds on a DC 10 Wisdom (Medicine) check.

name: Lightly Obscured
description: You have Disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks to see something in a Lightly Obscured space.

name: Line
description: Area of Effect. A Line extends from a point of origin in a straight path along its length and covers an area defined by its width.

name: Long Jump
description: Leap horizontally up to your Strength score in feet if you move at least 10 feet first. Standing: half distance. Each foot costs a foot of movement. Landing in Difficult Terrain: DC 10 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check or Prone.

name: Long Rest
description: At least 8 hours of downtime (6 hours sleep, 2 hours light activity). Benefits: Regain all HP and Hit Dice; ability scores and HP max restored; Exhaustion reduced by 1. Interrupted by: rolling Initiative, casting non-cantrip spells, taking damage, or 1 hour of physical exertion. Must wait 16 hours before starting another.

name: Magic
description: Action. Cast a spell with a casting time of an action or use a feature/magic item requiring a Magic action. For spells with casting time of 1 minute+, you must take the Magic action each turn and maintain Concentration.

name: Magical Effect
description: An effect is magical if created by a spell, a magic item, or a phenomenon that a rule labels as magical.

name: Malnutrition
description: Hazard. Food needs per day: Tiny 1/4 lb, Small 1 lb, Medium 1 lb, Large 4 lb, Huge 16 lb, Gargantuan 64 lb. Eating less than half: DC 10 Constitution save or gain 1 Exhaustion level. No food for 5 days: automatic Exhaustion.

name: Monster
description: A monster is a creature controlled by the GM, even if the creature is benevolent.

name: Nonplayer Character
description: A nonplayer character (NPC) is a monster that has a personal name and a distinct personality.

name: Object
description: An object is a nonliving, distinct thing. Composite things, like buildings, comprise more than one object.

name: Occupied Space
description: A space is occupied if a creature is in it or if it is completely filled by objects.

name: Opportunity Attack
description: When a creature you can see leaves your reach, you can take a Reaction to make one melee attack with a weapon or Unarmed Strike against it. The attack occurs right before it leaves your reach.

name: Paralyzed
description: Condition. Incapacitated. Speed 0. Auto-fail Strength and Dexterity saves. Attack rolls against you have Advantage. Hits within 5 feet are Critical Hits.

name: Passive Perception
description: Equals 10 + Wisdom (Perception) check bonus. Advantage: +5. Disadvantage: -5.

name: Per Day
description: If a rule says you can use something a certain number of times per day, you must finish a Long Rest to use it again after running out.

name: Petrified
description: Condition. Transformed into solid inanimate substance (usually stone). Weight x10, cease aging. Incapacitated. Speed 0. Auto-fail Strength and Dex saves. Attack rolls against you have Advantage. Resistance to all damage. Immunity to Poisoned condition.

name: Player Character
description: A player character is a character controlled by a player.

name: Poisoned
description: Condition. Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

name: Possession
description: Some effects cause a creature to be possessed. Prevented by Protection from Evil and Good, ended by Dispel Evil and Good.

name: Proficiency
description: If you have proficiency with something, you can add your Proficiency Bonus to any D20 Test you make using that thing.

name: Prone
description: Condition. Movement options: crawl or spend half Speed to stand up. Disadvantage on attack rolls. Attacks within 5 feet have Advantage against you; otherwise Disadvantage.

name: Reach
description: A creature has a reach of 5 feet unless a rule says otherwise.

name: Reaction
description: A special action taken in response to a trigger. Can be taken on another creature's turn. Once used, can't take another until the start of your next turn.

name: Ready
description: Action. Wait for a specific circumstance, then act with a Reaction before your next turn. Choose the trigger and the response action or movement. Readied spells require Concentration to hold.

name: Resistance
description: Damage of that type is halved (round down). Applied only once to an instance of damage.

name: Restrained
description: Condition. Speed 0. Attack rolls against you have Advantage, your attack rolls have Disadvantage. Disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.

name: Ritual
description: If you have a spell prepared with the Ritual tag, you can cast it as a Ritual. Takes 10 minutes longer. Doesn't expend a spell slot. Can't be cast at a higher level.

name: Round Down
description: Whenever you divide or multiply a number in the game, round down if you end up with a fraction, unless a rule says to round up.

name: Save
description: Save is another name for a saving throw.

name: Saving Throw
description: Represents an attempt to avoid or resist a threat. You can decide to fail without rolling. If a target lacks the required ability score, it automatically fails.

name: Search
description: Action. Make a Wisdom check. Skills: Insight (state of mind), Medicine (ailment/cause of death), Perception (concealed creature/object), Survival (tracks/food).

name: Shape-Shifting
description: Any ongoing effects carry over from one form to the other unless stated otherwise. You revert to your true form if you die.

name: Short Rest
description: 1-hour downtime. Must have at least 1 HP to start. Spend Hit Dice to regain HP. Interrupted by: rolling Initiative, casting non-cantrip spells, or taking damage. Interrupted rest confers no benefits.

name: Simultaneous Effects
description: If two or more things happen at the same time on a turn, the person whose turn it is decides the order.

name: Size
description: Categories: Tiny, Small, Medium, Large, Huge, Gargantuan. Determines space occupied in combat.

name: Skill
description: A skill is an area of specialization associated with an ability check. Add Proficiency Bonus if proficient.

name: Speed
description: The distance in feet a creature can cover when it moves on its turn. Special speeds (Burrow, Climb, Fly, Swim) can be switched during movement by subtracting distance already moved from new speed. Changes to Speed affect all special speeds equally.

name: Spell
description: A spell is a magical effect that has the characteristics described in the Spells rules.

name: Spell Attack
description: A spell attack is an attack roll made as part of a spell or another magical effect.

name: Spellcasting Focus
description: An object used in place of a spell's Material components if those materials aren't consumed and don't have a cost specified.

name: Sphere
description: Area of Effect. Extends from a point of origin outward in all directions. The point of origin is included in the area of effect.

name: Stable
description: A creature is Stable if it has 0 Hit Points but isn't required to make Death Saving Throws.

name: Stat Block
description: Contains the game statistics of a monster: Size, Creature Type, Alignment, AC/Initiative/HP, Speed, Ability Scores, Skills, Resistances/Vulnerabilities, Immunities, Gear, Senses, Languages, CR, Traits, Actions, Bonus Actions, Reactions.

name: Study
description: Action. Make an Intelligence check. Areas of Knowledge: Arcana (spells, magic items, planes), History (events, civilizations, wars), Investigation (traps, ciphers, riddles), Nature (terrain, flora, weather), Religion (deities, rites, holy symbols, cults).

name: Stunned
description: Condition. Incapacitated. Auto-fail Strength and Dexterity saves. Attack rolls against you have Advantage.

name: Suffocation
description: Hazard. Hold breath for 1 + Constitution modifier minutes (min 30 seconds). When out of breath, gain 1 Exhaustion level at end of each turn. Breathing again removes all suffocation Exhaustion.

name: Surprise
description: If caught unawares at the start of combat, you have Disadvantage on your Initiative roll.

name: Swimming
description: Each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot (2 extra in Difficult Terrain). Ignored with Swim Speed. Rough water: DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check.

name: Swim Speed
description: A Swim Speed can be used to swim without expending the extra movement normally associated with swimming.

name: Target
description: The creature or object targeted by an attack roll, forced to make a saving throw, or selected to receive the effects of a spell or phenomenon.

name: Telepathy
description: A magical ability to communicate mentally within a specified range. The contacted creature must understand at least one language or be telepathic. No line of sight needed. Broken if either creature is Incapacitated or out of range.

name: Teleportation
description: Disappear and reappear elsewhere instantly without moving through intervening space. Doesn't expend movement or provoke Opportunity Attacks. Equipment teleports with you. If destination is occupied, appear in nearest unoccupied space.

name: Temporary Hit Points
description: Granted by certain effects and act as a buffer against losing real Hit Points.

name: Tremorsense
description: Can pinpoint creatures and moving objects within a specific range if both are in contact with the same surface or liquid. Can't detect creatures in the air. Doesn't count as sight.

name: Truesight
description: Enhanced vision within a specified range. Pierces: Darkness (normal and magical), Invisibility, Visual Illusions (appear transparent, auto-succeed on saves), Transformations (see true form), Ethereal Plane.

name: Unarmed Strike
description: A melee attack using your body. Options: Damage (attack roll, bonus = Str mod + PB, deals 1 + Str mod Bludgeoning), Grapple (target Str/Dex save, DC 8 + Str mod + PB, gives Grappled condition, must have free hand, target no more than one size larger), Shove (target Str/Dex save, same DC, push 5 feet or cause Prone condition).

name: Unconscious
description: Condition. Incapacitated and Prone, drop everything held. Speed 0. Attack rolls against you have Advantage. Auto-fail Strength and Dex saves. Hits within 5 feet are Critical Hits. Unaware of surroundings.

name: Unoccupied Space
description: A space is unoccupied if no creatures are in it and it isn't completely filled by objects.

name: Utilize
description: Action. When an object requires an action for its use, you take the Utilize action.

name: Vulnerability
description: Damage of that type is doubled against you. Applied only once to an instance of damage.

name: Weapon
description: An object in the Simple or Martial weapon category.

name: Weapon Attack
description: A weapon attack is an attack roll made with a weapon.
